critic_raw:
Issue ICU-1 — Policy-Dependent Outcome: The transfer endpoint is shaped by bed availability, escalation thresholds, and two documented policy changes during the study window. The model cannot be said to predict clinical decline when the label is a mediated institutional decision.

Issue ICU-2 — Unreported Site-Stratified Analysis: The two sites have demonstrably different workflows and mid-study changes. Pooling without stratification makes the aggregate performance figures uninterpretable.

Issue ICU-3 — Overstatement of Generalizability: The conclusion that the model should guide ward escalation workflows is not supported by the evidence. There is no demonstration of site-level validity, no physiologic endpoint, and no threshold analysis.
defender_raw:
Pass 1 — Analysis (Critic output visible):
ICU-1: Valid. Transfer is policy-mediated and two documented changes occurred during the evaluation window. The comparison to the prior score is internally consistent but the clinical decline framing is not.
ICU-2: Valid. Site stratification is absent and needed.
ICU-3: Valid. The deployment conclusion generalizes beyond what the pooled retrospective evidence supports.

Pass 2 — Verdicts: All three conceded. The deployment recommendation in particular goes well beyond what the evidence can support.

Overall: critique_wins.

debate_rounds.note: Defender conceded all points at first pass. No debate rounds required.

adjudication_raw:
ADJUDICATION — hidden_confounding_icu_transfer_207 — Multi-Round Run 3

Consistent with Runs 1 and 2. All three issues conceded by defender without requiring debate rounds. The structural flaws — policy-dependent label, missing site stratification, unsupported clinical decline claim — are unambiguous given the manuscript description.

Verdict: CRITIQUE_WINS
